title: quercus ilex f. vulgaris
intro: Quercus ilex f. vulgaris, also known as the holm oak or common holm oak, is a subspecies of evergreen tree in the beech family native to the Mediterranean region. The leaves and bark of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with medicinal properties attributed to their high tannin content, which acts as an astringent and anti-inflammatory agent. The health benefits of Quercus ilex f. vulgaris include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Holm oak preparations often include teas, tinctures, and infusions of the leaves and bark, which are used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
